rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluoro-selenenylation of α,β-unsaturated esters and related electron-deficient olefins using PhSeSePh and Et3N·5HF as fluoride source in an undivided cell to give vicinal β-fluoro-α-phenylselenyl (and regioisomeric α-fluoro-β-phenylselenyl) esters.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh in the presence of fluoride generates an electrophilic [PhSeF] species, which adds to the olefin to form a seleniranium (episelenonium) cation that is opened by fluoride.

key_intermediates.name: α-PhSe-stabilized β-carbocation intermediate (more stable for 1a, leading to 2a)
β-PhSe / α-carbocation intermediate A (minor for 1a, leading to 3a)
Seleniranium ion from crotonate (1c) opened to α-cation stabilized by α-PhSe (intermediate B)
Seleniranium from methacrylate (1b) opened to β-cation stabilized by α-methyl (more stable, leading to 3b)

electron_flow: At the anode, PhSeSePh is oxidized in the presence of F− (from Et3N·5HF) to give an electrophilic [PhSeF] equivalent; this adds to the α,β-unsaturated ester to form a seleniranium ion, which is opened regioselectively by F− at the carbon best able to stabilize positive charge (α-PhSe stabilization or α-alkyl stabilization), delivering the vicinal F/SePh adduct.
notes: Regiochemistry is rationalized by relative stability of the cationic intermediate arising from seleniranium opening: with simple acrylate (1a) α-PhSe stabilization dominates giving 2a as major (β-F, α-SePh); with α-methyl substrates (methacrylate 1b) the tertiary β-carbocation stabilized by the α-methyl is favored, inverting regioselectivity toward 3b (α-F, β-SePh); crotonate (1c) gives exclusively 2c. No radical traps, CV, or DFT reported in these pages; mechanism is inferred from product distributions and substituent effects.
